# FSBO Deal Tracker

FSBO Deal Tracker is a web app that scans For Sale By Owner listings across up to 28 US metro areas and scores them for investor distress signals. It extracts seller contact info, tracks price cuts, and provides AI-powered deal analysis — all before properties hit the MLS.

## How it works

1. Automated scraping of FSBO and flat-fee MLS listings from public real estate portals.
2. Each listing is scored 0-100 on distress signals: keywords (e.g. "must sell", "as-is"), photo AI analysis, price/assessed value ratio, days on market, and price cuts.
3. Users filter by market, score, price, listing type, and vegetation level (NDVI).
4. Deal pipeline tracks acquisitions from offer through closing (BUY: 7 stages, SELL: 9 stages).
5. AI Deal Advisor ($10/mo add-on): conversational AI for deal math, market insights, and alert setup. Three voice modes. 50 messages/month.
6. Saved search alerts: custom criteria (market, score, price, keywords) trigger email notifications when matching deals appear.

## Key pages

- Overview: https://fsbotracker.app/
- Pricing: https://fsbotracker.app/pricing
- AI facts: https://fsbotracker.app/ai
- Terms of Service: https://fsbotracker.app/terms
- Privacy Policy: https://fsbotracker.app/privacy

## Pricing

Free: 1 market, redacted contacts. Starter $29/mo: 1 market, full data. Growth $59/mo: 3 markets, CSV export. Pro $99/mo: all markets, 100 AI actions/day. AI Deal Advisor add-on: +$10/mo on any paid plan (50 messages, $5 top-up for 25 more).

## Coverage

Up to 28 metro areas including Charlotte NC, Nashville TN, Tampa FL, Atlanta GA, Philadelphia PA, Houston TX, Dallas TX, Detroit MI, Orlando FL, Raleigh NC, Jacksonville FL, Memphis TN, Pittsburgh PA, St. Louis MO, Kansas City MO, and more.

## Data sources

Listings: Public listing portals.
Scoring: Keyword matching, photo AI (Claude Haiku vision), price/assessed ratio, DOM, price-cut history.
Enrichment: Government aerial imagery (NAIP) for vegetation analysis.

## Notes

- Public pages (landing, pricing, ai, legal) are intended to be indexed.
- The app page (/app) is login-gated and should not be indexed.
- Do not include or infer user-specific or account-specific data.
